title: "What’s That Bird? — A 2-day birdwatching workshop for beginners & curious minds!
caption_full:
108 likes, 4 comments - dinkarsamore on June 30, 2026: "What’s That Bird? — 
A 2-day birdwatching workshop for beginners & curious minds! 

This 2-day workshop is designed to introduce you to the wonderful world of birds, birdwatching as a hobby, and help you identify species commonly seen around Jaipur city.

At Kishan Bagh Sand Dunes Park, Jaipur | On 4th & 5th July | Between 6:30–8:30 AM | Walks, ID tips, & more

Limited seats available! To register & know more, Dm or WhatsApp me: 7014563426".

title: Movie Screening - Dug Dug
caption_full:
The AIFF Show is back this Friday, 3rd July, 5 PM, at Science Park Auditorium, and this one’s big.
We’re hosting Ritwik Pareek, director, Prerna Pareek, producer and the entire team of Dug Dug . A film about a motorcycle shrine and a Rajasthani roadside legend that went all the way to the Toronto International Film Festival, and now it’s coming back home to Jaipur where the story actually began.
Catch the screening, hear the team talk about how a local legend turned into a film that travelled the world.
3rd July | 5 PM | Science Park Auditorium
See you there. #filmfestival #jaipur #filmscreening #dugdug #aiff @ritwikpareek @anuragkashyap10 @motwayne @nikkhiladvani @vasanbala @cinemakasam @papihah @balabijith @actoryogendra9 @gauravsoni @salvageaudiocollective @rhnr @thejjsticks @viraajsaxena @studirt @theprodro @mainhoonromy @andreas_brueckl_colorist @ankurtewari @doobsey @rhitz_xcurve_audio @filmyaroon @raghuveersinghrathore1 @nothing.shekhar @verma_kshitij @neejudge @rajendraakula @filmynerd @mulberry_media @ohflipfilms @dugdugthefilm @ranjit1979 @prernakathuria @filmyaroon @yashikaroutray @suryadevpareek".

title: Weekend Movie Screenings by Jaipur Center For Arts
venue: Jaipur Center for Arts, City Palace
caption_full:
186 likes, 4 comments - jaipurcentreforart on June 29, 2026: "🎬 The wait is over! We’re happy to share the opening weekend schedule for our Summer Film Programme: Artists’ Cinema / Artist’s Cinema, Curated by Shwetal A. Patel @shwetalashvinpatel 

Starting this Friday, 3 July, at Jaipur Centre for Art, City Palace, Jaipur, the programme brings together a wide range of films from India and around the world.

Running from 3 July to 9 August, the programme features 150+ films, including artists’ films, independent cinema, restored classics, animation, and student films.

📽️ Daily screenings from 2:00 PM, with the last screening ending around 8:00 PM. 

Whether you’re a film lover, student, artist, or simply curious to discover something new, we hope you’ll join us.

Register via the link in our bio. Limited seats available.
Swipe to view the full schedule.

@ali.cherri @studio.ainak @suroorchi @rintuthomas @mentalsyrup @forgetfulblink @appuso_man @don.palathara @amshuchukki @poorna_s @alreem.albeshr @siva.ranjini @arjuna_film @yashasvijuyal @sraiyanti @sudarshanstudio 

#ArtistsCinema #SummerFilmProgramme #JaipurCentreForArt #CityPalaceJaipur #ContemporaryCinema 

[ArtAndCinema, IndependentCinema FilmScreening, Jaipur, City Palace, Jaipur Centre for Art, Museum, Films, Art, indie Cinema]".

title: Heritage Photo Walk and Photography Workshop at City Palace
caption_full:
"Led by The Ethereal Circuit, in collaboration with Canon, we are delighted to invite you to a Heritage Photo Walk and Photography Workshop at the City Palace Museum, Jaipur.

This session offers a chance to wander through the Palace’s magnificent spaces, observing its architectural and cultural details while learning to capture them through the lens. Participants will explore composition, framing, and storytelling in an atmosphere that celebrates heritage and creativity.

We look forward to welcoming you. Please register via the link in bio.

Where: City Palace Museum, Jaipur
When: Saturday, July 4, 2026
Time: 4:30 PM – 6:30 PM
Registration: First‑come, first‑served basis | ₹300 per participant".
